title: Dialect-Aware NLP
subtitle: Developing NLP solutions for Kurdish dialectal variation - and beyond

sections.heading: Overview
sections.value: Dialect-Aware NLP is the working title for my PhD research at Saarland University. The core question is: how do we build NLP systems that do not merely tolerate dialectal variation but actively account for it? The project takes Kurdish as its primary testbed - a language family with extensive, well-documented dialectal diversity and very limited digital resources - and develops modular, transferable solutions intended to generalise to other dialect-rich, low-resource language situations.

sections.heading: Why Kurdish?
sections.value: Kurdish is an ideal research language for dialect-aware NLP for several reasons. It has a large number of speakers across multiple countries, yet is severely underrepresented in NLP benchmarks and tools. Its dialects - Kurmanji, Sorani, Zazaki, Gorani, and many sub-varieties within each - are sometimes mutually unintelligible, yet existing NLP resources conflate them into a single 'Kurdish' label. This makes Kurdish a challenging and scientifically interesting case study, and any improvements directly benefit a real community of speakers.

sections.heading: Research Threads
items: Dialectal Filtering: deriving dialect-specific sub-corpora from large noisy, mixed-dialect text collections.
Kurdish Voices: dialect-aware fine-tuning of ASR models for spoken Kurdish varieties.
Kurdish Interpretability: using mechanistic interpretability to understand what language models learn about Kurdish dialects.
Dialect Mapping: building a comprehensive geographic and typological map of Kurdish dialectal variation.
Kobani Analysis: in-depth phonological and morphological study of the Kobani variety as a case study.

sections.heading: Long-Term Vision
sections.value: The methods developed for Kurdish are designed to be language-agnostic wherever possible. The long-term goal is a transferable methodology for dialect-aware NLP that can be applied to other dialect-rich, low-resource language families - particularly those where dialect variation is linguistically significant but computationally ignored.

sections.heading: Current Status
sections.value: This is an active PhD project. Results from several of the research threads have already been presented at international conferences (ICKL-6, ICKL-7). Further publications and updates will be added here as the work progresses. [TODO: add links to papers and talks as they are published]
